subject: Statistics
title: Backtesting and Cross-Validation
description: A fast breakdown of Backtesting and Cross-Validation for builders.

hook.text: Does your model actually forecast well? Train it on 2010-2019, forecast 2020, and check. That's backtesting.

buildup.text: Time series cross-validation slides the training window forward, forecasting each period from all prior data.

discovery.text: Unlike regular cross-validation, time series must respect the order. You can never train on future data.

twist.text: A model that backtests well on stable periods may fail during crises. The past doesn't always represent the future.

climax.text: Rolling-origin evaluation gives the most honest assessment of real-world forecast accuracy.

punchline.text: Test on the past before predicting the future. But the future may differ.

quiz.question: Why must time series cross-validation respect chronological order?
options: Training on future data would give unrealistically optimistic accuracy estimates
It doesn't matter — any data order works
Only the most recent data is relevant
